reviews.title: We're going over the mountain, Jerry
reviews.body: Time and time again, when it's either too much from the night before, or too much Uncle Cid swimmin around, this is the show, the only sound I can bear.... When you cannot hold your coffee mug, that acoustic set is as good as pain meds! When you are pacing and hallucinating, let Jerry take you over the mountain, the electric set is a journey, my friend. Having now heard so many shows that we didn't easily have back in the day, I can compare the cream of the crop. Hard to match a room full of college aged acid freaks mind melting en masse! Charlie Miller, you have the ears like Owsley...endless thanks to you!

reviews.title: Half-Acoustic, half-Electric, & Completely Dead
reviews.body: If you think for one second about the entirety of the Archive; millions of songs at your disposal, virtually everything seamless from phone to computer, and the ability to capture for yourself some semblance of a collection in an aging digital world. To wander aimlessly is absolute ignorance. You must intentionally decide on carrying through the mystery web with some kind of light, otherwise you are bound to get lost in a parade of decadence. Relax...you have managed to find one of those tapes which actually drip magic from it's timbre. You've done it, now sit back and enjoy :) Peace To All Human Beings

reviews.title: Incredible quality, please please make some type of flac download
reviews.body: I know this will likely fall on deaf ears but I adore this set and this is legitimately the best recording on the internet of it, doubly so for the new riders' segment. I would love to be able to take it with me on long road trips and camping excursions devoid of internet, so please, dear archivist, please at least upload the new riders' set in a downloadable listing. All other recordings of it (unlike dicks' picks) are off of the radio broadcast and not even comparable in quality. God bless.

reviews.title: Warm to Hot Show
reviews.body: This is the real GD show. You got a intimate surrounding and the boys felt nice and comfy. Early acoustic GD with some help by the Riders. Then it is reversed with the Riders with some help from the Dead. " Thank you boys " Garcia telling the small crowd " we got you all night " Electric Dead comes on and away we go. Closing with Viola into We Bid You.... If you wanted a time capsule of what it was like in 1970 tour this is the show to go to. Relaxed, funny, cosmic , and funky . Have fun.

reviews.title: Seriously, Three Stars???
reviews.body: Just average 1970?? Just GOGD?? Dancin' never really lifts off? TIFTOO just a basic 70 run through? What planet are you living on? TIFTOO is the single best version I've ever heard, and so is Dancin' -- much better than the subsequent MIT rendition. VLB -- again, a candidate for best ever. There may be better Good Lovin's out there, but this one is excellent as well.
